data.body: Implemented epic 7c02 (configurable tracker dir + branch name) end-to-end across 5 children, each subagent-reviewed before close. c1 (14b8): TrackerConfig section + branch/dir validators + env alias + docs + validator unit tests. c2 (8f43): tracker_dir() routed through load_config, new tickets_branch() resolver, unified the reads.py duplicate (preserving its git work-tree gate) + tests. c4 (3e28): init creates/symlinks/excludes at the configured dir + AST guard test. c3 (4dde): all tickets/origin/tickets/HEAD:tickets branch literals across init/sync/push/reconciler/reads/fsck now resolve via tickets_branch() (tracker-only callers resolve from the main repo = tracker parent). c5 (5436): fsck configured-vs-mounted branch-mismatch WARN + dir-mismatch hint + hermetic e2e tests (custom dir+branch write+sync round-trip across two clones). Defaults byte-for-byte unchanged; custom branch verified end-to-end (auto-push lands on the configured remote branch). During final verification found+fixed a config-cache regression the early load_config calls exposed (a config file appearing where none was discovered was missed) — also fixes latent MCP staleness. Full suite: 2949 passed; only the pre-existing unrelated test_external_isolation (grounding) fails. Epic closed + completion-signed.
